site_goal: Present the centuries-old Worshipful Guild of Stringed-Instrument Luthiers as a living craft body — connecting prospective apprentices, member workshops, collectors, and the curious public to its members, materials lore, exhibitions, and technical journal.
description: An ornate editorial heritage site rendered as a leather-bound folio brought to the screen. The home page opens like the frontispiece of an 18th-century treatise: a centered engraved viola d'amore framed by acanthus scrollwork, with the guild's full Latin-and-English name set in spaced caps above a date roundel reading 'ESTABLISHED MDCCXIV'. Body copy runs in two narrow justified columns with hanging drop caps, ligatures (ct, st, fi, fl), and small-caps for proper nouns. The walnut-brown chrome (#4A2E1F) sits against a parchment ground (#F2E6D0) with deep burgundy (#6B1E2A) reserved for rules, ornamental dividers, and link underlines. Hand-engraved spot illustrations — a coiled scroll, a chisel, a maple billet showing flame figure — sit in the margins like a Diderot plate. Navigation is a thin parchment ribbon with a wax-seal hover state. Pages feel like chapters: each has a hand-set chapter number ('CAP. III'), a foliate header rule, and footnotes in italic. The instrument-of-the-month is presented as a tipped-in plate with a copperplate caption and provenance notes. The journal page mimics an engraved scientific periodical with figure numbers and roman-numeraled sections.
motif: engraved frontispiece with acanthus scrollwork, wax seals, foliate rules, and tipped-in plate illustrations

pages.layout_pattern: engraved frontispiece: centered title cartouche, large engraved instrument, date roundel, then a two-column 'preface from the Warden' with drop caps, followed by three plate-card teasers (Apprenticeship / Exhibition / Journal) and a footer with the guild oath
sections: title cartouche with full guild name and MDCCXIV roundel
centered engraved hero instrument (viola d'amore) within acanthus frame
preface from the Warden — two justified columns with drop cap
three plate-card teasers linking to Apprenticeship, Exhibition, Journal
instrument-of-the-month teaser strip with tipped-in plate
footer with guild oath in small caps and arms of the guild

pages.layout_pattern: engraved directory: filter ribbon across the top, then a grid of workshop cards each shaped like a tradesman's sign with the workshop's house mark, master luthier, city, and specialties; a foldout map of Europe shows pin locations as small engraved instruments
sections: directory intro paragraph in italic
filter ribbon (instrument family / region / accepting apprentices)
workshop card grid with engraved house marks
engraved foldout map of member locations
'Become a member workshop' call-to-action with application steps

pages.layout_pattern: chapter-style page: CAP. III header rule, opening paragraph with drop cap, then a three-tier vertical timeline (Apprentice → Journeyman → Master) with foliate dividers, expectations and stipend in side margins as footnotes, closing with an application form set as an engraved petition
sections: chapter heading 'CAP. III — Of the Path to Mastery'
opening paragraph with drop cap
three-tier timeline of apprentice / journeyman / master
marginal footnotes on stipend, hours at the bench, examination
engraved petition-style application form

pages.layout_pattern: Diderot-plate inspired catalogue: top half a large labelled engraving of an instrument cross-section with callouts, bottom half a sortable table of woods, varnishes, and strings with grading and sourcing partner; a sidebar offers a sustainability note from the guild forester
sections: labelled cross-section engraving with numbered callouts
sortable materials table (species, grading, sourcing partner, sustainability)
varnish recipe vignette with engraved bottles
forester's sidebar note in italic
interactive billet rotator (quarter-sawn / flat-sawn)

pages.layout_pattern: annual-exhibition gallery: top banner with year and host city in cartouche, hero plate of the Best-in-Show instrument, then a two-column gallery of juried instruments with copperplate captions; below, an archive carousel of past years' frontispieces
sections: year and host city cartouche
Best-in-Show hero plate with juror citation
two-column gallery of juried instruments
list of attending workshops
archive carousel of past exhibition frontispieces

pages.layout_pattern: tipped-in plate spread: full-bleed engraved instrument on the verso, copperplate caption and detailed entry on the recto with maker, year, dimensions, varnish, tonal notes, and a provenance chain styled as a numbered lineage; audio sample plays from a phonograph-horn button
sections: tipped-in plate of the instrument (verso)
copperplate caption and full entry (recto)
provenance chain as a numbered lineage
audio sample with phonograph-horn play control
'previous featured instruments' thumbnail strip

pages.layout_pattern: engraved periodical: masthead with roman-numeraled volume and issue, table of contents with article titles and authors, then the lead article rendered in two columns with figure numbers, footnotes, and an author colophon; sidebar lists past issues
sections: masthead with volume / issue in Roman numerals
table of contents
lead article in two-column justified setting with figures and footnotes
author colophon with engraved portrait
past issues sidebar

message_intent: convey institutional gravitas and continuous lineage since 1714
demystify the path into the craft
establish authority on materials, technique, and provenance
celebrate the current month's featured instrument
required_text: The Worshipful Guild of Stringed-Instrument Luthiers
content_model: member workshop entries with workshop name, master luthier, city, specialties (violin family / lute family / guitar family / historical reproductions), founded year, contact, and an engraved house mark
apprenticeship program entries with tier (apprentice / journeyman / master), duration, prerequisites, stipend, hosting workshop, and application window
material entries with species (European spruce, Bosnian maple, Madagascan ebony, etc.), grading, sourcing partner, sustainability note, and engraved cross-section figure
exhibition entries with year, host city, juried winners, attending workshops, and plate gallery
instrument-of-the-month entry with maker, year, dimensions, varnish recipe, tonal notes, and provenance chain
journal articles with chapter heading, figures (numbered), footnotes, and author colophon
interactions: wax-seal hover state on nav items that 'presses' a burgundy seal beneath the label
workshop directory filter chips (instrument family / region / accepting apprentices) styled as engraved tags
interactive maple-billet rotator on the materials page that flips between flat-sawn and quarter-sawn engravings
footnote pop-overs on journal articles that surface the cited reference in a parchment tooltip
page-turn transition with a soft paper crease between chapters
asset_needs: high-detail engraved illustrations of: violin, viola d'amore, theorbo, classical guitar, scroll, f-hole, chisel set, maple billet cross-section
ornamental rule and corner-piece set (foliate, acanthus)
engraved house marks for ~12 member workshops
copperplate-style portrait engravings of three featured master luthiers
tipped-in plate frame ornament for the instrument-of-the-month
mobile_behavior: Two-column folio collapses to a single justified column; chapter numerals shrink to inline kickers; margin engravings reflow above their related paragraph as half-width plates; the wax-seal nav becomes a hamburger that opens a parchment drawer.
